title: Documentation for image save does not show some formats as not loadable
body:
### Issue Description

Trying to use Podman images in an air gapped environment (with Podman in a connected environment used to create & populate the images) _with limited sizes on the transport medium between_ the natural workflow is:

_In Connected Environment_

1. `podman create` & populate image some_name from Containerfile.
2. `podman save --format docker-dir -o local_dir some_name` _as per the example in help/docs
3. move to media for transport as individual files, (may require multiple media items).

_In Air Gapped Environment_

1. Load from transport media to local directory
2. `podman load -i local_dir`

Results in an error: `Error: remote client supports archives only but "local_dir" is a directory`
However the help/documents for load only mention tar file archives & creating such an archive from the directory contents does **not** result in a loadable archive.

While it would be nice if `podman load` supported all formats supported by `podman save` it seems misleading not to mention in the podman save help/documentation which formats **can** be used for `podman load` and especially not to give as an example a format that cannot be loaded.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. cd to a temporary directory
2. `podman save --format docker-dir quay.io/podman/hello -o hello_dir`
3. `podman load -i hello_dir`
`Error: remote client supports archives only but "hello_dir" is a directory`
4. Try as an archive: `cd hello_dir && python -mtarfile -c ../hello.tar . && cd ..`
5. `podman load -i hello.tar`
```
Error: unable to load image: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: open /var/tmp/libpod-images-load.tar2250068824/index.json: not a directory
 * oci-archive: loading index: open /var/tmp/container_images_oci4018169079/index.json: no such file or directory
 * docker-archive: decoding tar manifest.json: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []tarfile.Mani
festItem
 * dir: open /var/tmp/libpod-images-load.tar2250068824/manifest.json: not a directory
```
Archiving the directory hello_dir from the level above produces a near identical error message, _(temp paths differ)_.

### Describe the results you received

Error on trying to load items saved as docker-dir or oci-dir.

While it looks like I may be able to use something like:
   save to archive, unarchive or split to fit transport, reassemble, load from archive



### Describe the results you expected

Expected `podman load` to work for all of the formats documented in `podman save` **or** help/documentation for `podman save` to indicate to indicate which formats can be used for what.

### podman info output

```yaml
podman version 5.7.1
Documents checked latest online versions.
Tests run on Windows 11 & 10.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Initial problem encountered running Rocky Linux under Oracle Virtual Machine but problem confirmed under Windows 11.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Hi @GadgetSteve 

This is a limitation of the podman remote client. It's documented in the [podman load documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.7.1/markdown/podman-load.1.html) but I agree this could be clearer.

~~~
The local client further supports loading an oci-dir or a docker-dir as created with podman save (1).
~~~

Reading the [containers-transports(5) documentation](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-transports.5.md#dirpath) has a warning for the docker-dir export type:

> An existing local directory path storing the manifest, layer tarballs and signatures as individual files. This is a non-standardized format, primarily useful for debugging or noninvasive container inspection.

So I don't think we will add support to the podman remote client to handle this type of `load` in the future.

Is there a reason you are not using one of the other methods of saving like `docker-archive` or `oci-archive`?

The reason for not, initially, using the archive formats was simply that transport to the air gapped system(s) is expected to be via cd or dvd and some of the archives would exceed the capacity of the media. Now that I know that I have to use an archive format I can split the archive and reassemble on the other end.

Thank you for the explanation, that sounds frustrating having to use a CD or DVD. 

I will work on a PR to make this clearer in the documentation.
@GadgetSteve could you please take a look at https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27961 and let me know if you have any feedback?

Thinking about it I think that a part of the confusion is that it does not seem completely clear to me that the Windows client _both desktop and cli_ is classed as a "remote" client _even though it is all running on a single machine_ presumably because it is remote calling WSL2 code. Is it possible that this could be made clearer?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Is it okay to take over this issue? @ninja-quokka

> Is it okay to take over this issue? [@ninja-quokka](https://github.com/ninja-quokka)

Apologies for the delay @Honny1 , I have updated the attached PR opened to resolve this issue! PTAL when you have time.
